description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build and test and a variable CONTAINER_IMAGE set to registry.gitlab.com/$CI_PROJECT_PATH. Use the docker:stable image and docker:dind service for all jobs, and a before_script that logs in to the registry with docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com. In the build stage add a build job (except master) that runs docker build --no-cache -t $CONTAINER_IMAGE/web ./php/ --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE/web:$CI_BUILD_REF --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE/web:latest and docker build --no-cache -t $CONTAINER_IMAGE/db ./sql/ --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE/db:$CI_BUILD_REF --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE/db:latest, then pushes each tag with docker push. Also in the build stage add a build-stable job (only master) that runs the same builds but adds a --tag ...:stable for both web and db images and pushes all three tags for each. In the test stage add an a11y job that runs docker run -d --name db $CONTAINER_IMAGE/db:$CI_BUILD_REF, then docker run -p 80:80 -d --link db:db --name web $CONTAINER_IMAGE/web:$CI_BUILD_REF, and finally docker run --rm -v $(pwd)/pa11y:/home/node/app --link web:web civicactions/docker-pa11y pa11y-ci --config ci.json with a coverage regex "passes":\d+,"errors":\d+,.
